item.title: Thomson Reuters faces growing pressure over US immigration work
item.description: Shareholders challenge work for ICE after ex-employee sues legal database provider

item.title: China to drive a weight-loss drug price war
item.description: The active ingredient semaglutide is starting to lose patent protection and China can produce GLP-1 related treatments at scale

item.title: Goldman president warns private credit funds are not marketed properly
item.description: John Waldron says vehicles lack ‘clarity that this is really not a liquid product’

item.title: Goldman traders wrongfooted as Iran war upended interest rate expectations
item.description: Wall Street bank’s rates desk was key driver behind unexpected drop in fixed-income revenue

item.title: UK business urges more action on energy bills as ministers set out £600mn scheme
item.description: Lobby groups say Iran war shock calls for government support for broad range of companies

item.title: US jury finds Live Nation monopolised concert ticket markets
item.description: Trial will enter new phase to determine penalties for the company after an earlier settlement avoided its break-up

item.title: Veteran investor Mark Mobius dies at 89
item.description: Renowned fund manager was a pioneer in emerging market investing

item.title: ‘Megadeals’ defy market fears and volatile geopolitics
item.description: Boards should heed the lessons of the last record surge in big M&A in 2015

item.title: Amazon satellite deal takes the battle of the billionaires into orbit
item.description: Bezos and Musk compete for extraterrestrial dominance

item.title: Saudi Arabia’s PIF on verge of cutting support for LIV Golf
item.description: Sovereign wealth fund reconsiders backing for lossmaking golf tour as Iran war prompts investment rethink

item.title: Ad agencies settle FTC probe over alleged boycott of online platforms
item.description: Publicis, WPP and Dentsu were accused of collusion by the watchdog

item.title: United plus American won’t make US airlines great again
item.description: A tie-up might create a genuine US flag carrier, but there is not much scope to cut prices for passengers

item.title: US crude exports hit record as Iran war disrupts Middle East supplies
item.description: Foreign sales could push up US petrol prices and increase political pressure on Trump administration to curtail exports

item.title: Monte dei Paschi shareholders vote to reinstate ousted boss after boardroom feud
item.description: Almost half of the investors that voted backed slate including Luigi Lovaglio in opposition to board

item.title: UK gas prices dip below pre-Iran war level in relief for Reeves
item.description: Concerns about a full-blown supply crisis recede as Asia cuts consumption

item.title: Reader callout: Do you worry you’re giving your children bad financial advice?
item.description: University, property, investments — some of the pillars of sound financial guidance are being tested

item.title: Making sense of a CEO:worker wage ratio with basic sums
item.description: Pay rises for the many, hair shirts for the few, or some combination thereof

item.title: Rheinmetall wins €300mn drone order from German armed forces
item.description: Deal comes after CEO belittles Ukraine drone industry by comparing it to playing with ‘Legos’

item.title: Rate setters face ‘double danger’ from Iran war and tariffs, Fed official warns
item.description: Chicago Federal Reserve president Austan Goolsbee says public may ‘misinterpret’ price rises as persistent

item.title: Israeli start-up makes world’s first lab-grown chocolate bar
item.description: Mondelez-backed Celleste Bio says its ‘cell-cultured’ cocoa butter is identical to traditional product

item.title: US banks make record buybacks on Trump’s looser rules and choppy markets
item.description: Six largest lenders spend $33bn on shares, smashing market forecasts

item.title: Allbirds is turning into an AI compute provider, because of course it is
item.description: Flipping the ‘Bird

item.title: Reeves should avoid big energy support package for households, IMF says
item.description: Top fund official says UK chancellor looks ‘very aligned’ with recommendation of targeted help in wake of Iran shock

item.title: Nutella maker Ferrero raided over possible EU competition breaches
item.description: Ferrero Rocher maker says it was target of unannounced inspections this week

item.title: Morgan Stanley profits jump 30% as Iran war drives trading boom
item.description: Results from two remaining big investment banks cap earnings season marked by gains from Wall Street traders

item.title: Norway’s Blastr is preferred bidder for former Liberty Steel’s SSUK
item.description: Former linchpin of Sanjeev Gupta’s empire was placed in compulsory liquidation

item.title: BBC to cut one in 10 jobs in radical downsizing
item.description: New director-general Matt Brittin to replace Tim Davie next month

item.title: Echoes of Nuremberg in a new set of corporate trials
item.description: Jailing of former Lafarge CEO highlights a broader push for corporate accountability

item.title: Banks’ soaring exposure to trading firms creating ‘inherent fragility’, warns S&P
item.description: Large investment banks have become increasingly reliant on markets financing, report says

item.title: FirstFT: China shares satellite technology with Iran
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: Trump’s ‘banana republic’ rhetoric and OpenAI’s new cyber security model
